description: SUN ALTITUDE SPLIT INTO TWO VALUES. Previously listed as 5,733 km (single value). Now shows 'Sun altitude (physical): < 4,200 km' and 'Sun altitude (observed/apparent): 5,733 km'. This may be an attempt to address our sun/firmament collision argument (WIN-020 area: sun at 5,733 km exceeds H(r) ≈ 4,200 km at that radius). By splitting into 'physical' vs 'apparent', they can claim the physical sun is below the firmament while the apparent position is a refraction effect.
analyst_priority: MEDIUM — potential response to our sun/firmament collision critique. Evaluate whether this resolves or just relocates the contradiction.
